docs.title: flet-audio-recorder
docs.text:
Adds audio recording support to Flet apps.

It is based on the record Flutter package.

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
This package supports the following platforms:

Platform Supported Windows ✅ macOS ✅ Linux ✅ iOS ✅ Android ✅ Web ✅

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
To install the flet-audio-recorder package and add it to your project dependencies:

uvpippoetry 
uv add flet-audio-recorder

pip install flet-audio-recorder  # (1)!
  1. After this, you will have to manually add this package to your requirements.txt or pyproject.toml.
poetry add flet-audio-recorder
Note

On Linux, encoding is provided by fmedia which must be installed separately.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
main.py
import flet as ft

import flet_audio_recorder as far


def main(page: ft.Page):
    page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER
    page.appbar = ft.AppBar(title=ft.Text("Audio Recorder"), center_title=True)

    path = "test-audio-file.wav"

    def show_snackbar(message):
        page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(ft.Text(message)))

    async def handle_start_recording(e):
        show_snackbar(f"Starting recording...")
        await audio_rec.start_recording_async(output_path=path)

    async def handle_stop_recording(e):
        output_path = await audio_rec.stop_recording_async()
        show_snackbar(f"Stopped recording. Output Path: {output_path}")
        if page.web and output_path is not None:
            page.launch_url(output_path)

    async def handle_list_devices(e):
        o = await audio_rec.get_input_devices_async()
        show_snackbar(f"Input Devices: {', '.join([d.label for d in o])}")

    async def handle_has_permission(e):
        try:
            status = await audio_rec.has_permission_async()
            show_snackbar(f"Audio Recording Permission status: {status}")
        except Exception as e:
            show_snackbar(f"Error checking permission: {e}")

    async def handle_pause(e):
        print(f"isRecording: {await audio_rec.is_recording_async()}")
        if await audio_rec.is_recording_async():
            await audio_rec.pause_recording_async()

    async def handle_resume(e):
        print(f"isPaused: {await audio_rec.is_paused_async()}")
        if await audio_rec.is_paused_async():
            await audio_rec.resume_recording_async()

    async def handle_audio_encoder_test(e):
        supports_wav = await audio_rec.is_supported_encoder_async(far.AudioEncoder.WAV)

    audio_rec = far.AudioRecorder(
        configuration=far.AudioRecorderConfiguration(encoder=far.AudioEncoder.WAV),
        on_state_change=lambda e: print(f"State Changed: {e.data}"),
    )
    page.services.append(audio_rec)

    page.add(
        ft.Button("Start Audio Recorder", on_click=handle_start_recording),
        ft.Button("Stop Audio Recorder", on_click=handle_stop_recording),
        ft.Button("List Devices", on_click=handle_list_devices),
        ft.Button("Pause Recording", on_click=handle_pause),
        ft.Button("Resume Recording", on_click=handle_resume),
        ft.Button("WAV Encoder Support", on_click=handle_audio_encoder_test),
        ft.Button(
            "Get Audio Recording Permission Status", on_click=handle_has_permission
        ),
    )


ft.run(main)

docs.title: AudioRecorder
docs.text:
Bases: Service

A control that allows you to record audio from your device.

This control can record audio using different audio encoders and also allows configuration of various audio recording parameters such as noise suppression, echo cancellation, and more.

Note

This control is non-visual and should be added to Page.services list before it can be used.

docs.title: configuration: AudioRecorderConfiguration = field(default_factory=lambda: AudioRecorderConfiguration())
docs.text: The default configuration of the audio recorder.

docs.title: data: Any = skip_field()
docs.text: Arbitrary data of any type that can be attached to a control.

docs.title: key: str | int | float | bool | ValueKey | ScrollKey | None = None
on_state_change: ft.OptionalEventHandler[AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent[AudioRecorder]] = None
docs.text:
Event handler that is called when the state of the audio recorder changes.

Event handler argument is of type AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent.

docs.title: page: Page | PageView | None
docs.text: The page (of type Page or PageView) to which this control belongs to.

docs.title: parent: BaseControl | None
docs.text:
The direct ancestor(parent) of this control.

It defaults to None and will only have a value when this control is mounted (added to the page tree).

The Page control (which is the root of the tree) is an exception - it always has parent=None.

docs.title: cancel_recording(timeout: float | None = 10)
docs.text:
Cancels the current audio recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: cancel_recording_async(timeout: float | None = 10)
docs.text:
Cancels the current audio recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: get_input_devices_async(timeout: float | None = 10) -> list[InputDevice]
docs.text:
Retrieves the available input devices for recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: A list of available input devices. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: has_permission_async(timeout: float | None = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Checks if the app has permission to record audio.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the app has permission, False otherwise. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: is_paused_async(timeout: float | None = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Checks whether the audio recorder is currently paused.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the recorder is paused, False otherwise. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: is_recording_async(timeout: float | None = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Checks whether the audio recorder is currently recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the recorder is currently recording, False otherwise. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: is_supported_encoder_async(encoder: AudioEncoder, timeout: float | None = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Checks if the given audio encoder is supported by the recorder.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION encoder

The audio encoder to check.

TYPE: AudioEncoder

timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if the encoder is supported, False otherwise. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: pause_recording(timeout: float | None = 10)
docs.text:
Pauses the ongoing audio recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: pause_recording_async(timeout: float | None = 10)
docs.text:
Pauses the ongoing audio recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: resume_recording(timeout: float | None = 10)
docs.text:
Resumes a paused audio recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: resume_recording_async(timeout: float | None = 10)
docs.text:
Resumes a paused audio recording.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: start_recording_async(output_path: str | None = None, configuration: AudioRecorderConfiguration | None = None, timeout: float | None = 10) -> bool
docs.text:
Starts recording audio and saves it to the specified output path.

If not on the web, the output_path parameter must be provided.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION output_path

The file path where the audio will be saved. It must be specified if not on web.

TYPE: str | None DEFAULT: None

configuration

The configuration for the audio recorder. If None, the AudioRecorder.configuration will be used.

TYPE: AudioRecorderConfiguration | None DEFAULT: None

timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: True if recording was successfully started, False otherwise. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: stop_recording_async(timeout: float | None = 10) -> str | None
docs.text:
Stops the audio recording and optionally returns the path to the saved file.

PARAMETER DESCRIPTION timeout

The maximum amount of time (in seconds) to wait for a response.

TYPE: float | None DEFAULT: 10

Returns: The file path where the audio was saved or None if not applicable. Raises: TimeoutError: If the request times out.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Deployed online documentation: https://flet-dev.github.io/flet-audiorecorder/
  • AudioRecorder control new property: configuration
  • New dataclasses:
    • AudioRecorderConfiguration
    • AndroidRecorderConfiguration
    • IosRecorderConfiguration
    • InputDevice
  • New enums:
    • AndroidAudioSource
    • IosAudioCategoryOption

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Refactored AudioRecorder control to use @ft.control dataclass-style definition and switched to Service control type

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
• AudioRecorder must now be added to Page.services instead of Page.overlay.
  • Recording configuration properties (audio_encoder, suppress_noise, cancel_echo, auto_gain, channels_num, sample_rate, bit_rate) are now grouped under configuration: AudioRecorderConfiguration
  • Event on_state_changed renamed to on_state_change
  • In all methods, parameter wait_timeout was renamed to timeout.
  • The following sync methods were removed from AudioRecorder (use corresponding async methods instead):
    • is_recording → is_recording_async
    • stop_recording → stop_recording_async
    • cancel_recording → cancel_recording_async
    • resume_recording → resume_recording_async
    • pause_recording → pause_recording_async
    • is_paused → is_paused_async
    • is_supported_encoder → is_supported_encoder_async
    • get_input_devices → get_input_devices_async
    • has_permission → has_permission_async
Initial release.

docs.title: AndroidAudioSource
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Android-specific audio source types.

docs.title: CAMCORDER = 'camcorder'
docs.text: Microphone audio source tuned for video recording, with the same orientation as the camera, if available.

docs.title: DEFAULT_SOURCE = 'defaultSource'
docs.text: Default audio source.

docs.title: MIC = 'mic'
docs.text: Microphone audio source.

docs.title: REMOTE_SUBMIX = 'remoteSubMix'
docs.text: Audio source for a submix of audio streams to be presented remotely.

docs.title: UNPROCESSED = 'unprocessed'
docs.text: Microphone audio source tuned for unprocessed (raw) sound if available, behaves like DEFAULT_SOURCE otherwise.

docs.title: VOICE_CALL = 'voiceCall'
docs.text: Voice call uplink + downlink audio source.

docs.title: VOICE_COMMUNICATION = 'voiceCommunication'
docs.text: Microphone audio source tuned for voice communications such as VoIP.

docs.title: VOICE_DOWNLINK = 'voiceDownlink'
docs.text: Voice call downlink (Rx) audio source.

docs.title: VOICE_PERFORMANCE = 'voicePerformance'
docs.text: Source for capturing audio meant to be processed in real time and played back for live performance (e.g karaoke).

docs.title: VOICE_RECOGNITION = 'voiceRecognition'
docs.text: Microphone audio source tuned for voice recognition.

docs.title: VOICE_UPLINK = 'voiceUplink'
docs.text: Voice call uplink (Tx) audio source.

docs.title: AndroidRecorderConfiguration
docs.text: Android specific configuration for recording.

docs.title: audio_source: AndroidAudioSource = AndroidAudioSource.DEFAULT_SOURCE
docs.text:
Defines the audio source.

An audio source defines both a default physical source of audio signal, and a recording configuration. Some effects are available or not depending on this source.

Most of the time, you should use AndroidAudioSource.DEFAULT_SOURCE or AndroidAudioSource.MIC.

docs.title: manage_bluetooth: bool = True
docs.text: Try to start a bluetooth audio connection to a headset (Bluetooth SCO).

docs.title: mute_audio: bool = False
docs.text:
Whether to mute all audio streams like alarms, music, ring, etc.

This is useful when you want to record audio without any background noise. The streams are restored to their previous state after recording is stopped and will stay at current state on pause/resume.

docs.title: use_legacy: bool = False
docs.text:
Whether to use the Android MediaRecorder.

While advanced recorder (the default) unlocks additionnal features, the legacy recorder is stability oriented.

docs.title: AudioRecorderConfiguration
docs.text: Recording configuration.

docs.title: android_configuration: AndroidRecorderConfiguration = field(default_factory=lambda: AndroidRecorderConfiguration())
docs.text: Android specific configuration.

docs.title: auto_gain: bool = False
docs.text:
The recorder will try to auto adjust recording volume in a limited range (if available on the device).

Recording volume may be lowered by using this.

docs.title: bit_rate: ft.Number = 128000
docs.text: The audio encoding bit rate in bits per second if applicable.

docs.title: cancel_echo: bool = False
docs.text:
The recorder will try to reduce echo (if available on the device).

Recording volume may be lowered by using this.

docs.title: channels: int = 2
docs.text: The numbers of channels for the recording: 1 = mono, 2 = stereo Most platforms only accept 2 at most.

docs.title: device: InputDevice | None = None
docs.text: The device to be used for recording. If None, default device will be selected.

docs.title: encoder: AudioEncoder = AudioEncoder.WAV
docs.text: The requested output format through this given encoder.

docs.title: ios_configuration: IosRecorderConfiguration = field(default_factory=lambda: IosRecorderConfiguration())
docs.text: iOS specific configuration.

docs.title: sample_rate: int = 44100
docs.text: The sample rate for audio in samples per second if applicable.

docs.title: suppress_noise: bool = False
docs.text:
The recorder will try to negates the input noise (if available on the device).

Recording volume may be lowered by using this.

docs.title: AudioRecorderState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

State of the audio recorder.

docs.title: PAUSED = 'paused'
docs.text: The audio recorder is paused and can be resumed.

docs.title: RECORDING = 'recording'
docs.text: The audio recorder is currently recording audio.

docs.title: STOPPED = 'stopped'
docs.text: The audio recorder is stopped and not recording.

docs.title: AudioEncoder
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Represents the different audio encoders for audio recording.

docs.title: AACELD = 'aacEld'
docs.text: Advanced Audio Codec Enhanced Low Delay. Suitable for low-latency applications like VoIP.

docs.title: AACHE = 'aacHe'
docs.text: Advanced Audio Codec High Efficiency. Optimized for high-quality audio at lower bit rates.

docs.title: AACLC = 'aacLc'
docs.text: Advanced Audio Codec Low Complexity. A commonly used encoder for streaming and general audio recording.

docs.title: AMRNB = 'amrNb'
docs.text: Adaptive Multi-Rate Narrow Band. Used for speech audio in mobile communication.

docs.title: AMRWB = 'amrWb'
docs.text: Adaptive Multi-Rate Wide Band. Used for higher-quality speech audio.

docs.title: FLAC = 'flac'
docs.text: Free Lossless Audio Codec. Provides high-quality lossless audio compression.

docs.title: OPUS = 'opus'
docs.text: A codec designed for both speech and audio applications, known for its versatility.

docs.title: PCM16BITS = 'pcm16bits'
docs.text: Pulse Code Modulation with 16-bit depth, used for high-fidelity audio.

docs.title: WAV = 'wav'
docs.text: Standard audio format used for raw, uncompressed audio data.

docs.title: AudioRecorderState
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

State of the audio recorder.

docs.title: PAUSED = 'paused'
docs.text: The audio recorder is paused and can be resumed.

docs.title: RECORDING = 'recording'
docs.text: The audio recorder is currently recording audio.

docs.title: STOPPED = 'stopped'
docs.text: The audio recorder is stopped and not recording.

docs.title: AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent
docs.text: Bases: Event[EventControlType]

docs.title: state: AudioRecorderState
docs.text: The new state of the audio recorder.

docs.title: InputDevice
docs.text: An audio input device.

docs.title: id: str
docs.text: The ID used to select the device on the platform.

docs.title: label: str
docs.text: The label text representation.

docs.title: IosAudioCategoryOption
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Audio behaviors.

Each option is valid only for specific audio session categories.

docs.title: ALLOW_AIRPLAY = 'allowAirPlay'
docs.text:
Stream audio from this session to AirPlay devices.

Available from iOS 10.0.

docs.title: ALLOW_BLUETOOTH = 'allowBluetooth'
docs.text: Bluetooth hands-free devices appear as available input routes.

docs.title: ALLOW_BLUETOOTH_A2DP = 'allowBluetoothA2DP'
docs.text:
Stream audio from this session to Bluetooth devices that support the Advanced Audio Distribution Profile (A2DP).

Available from iOS 10.0.

docs.title: DEFAULT_TO_SPEAKER = 'defaultToSpeaker'
docs.text: Audio from the session defaults to the built-in speaker instead of the receiver.

docs.title: DUCK_OTHERS = 'duckOthers'
docs.text: Reduces the volume of other audio sessions while audio from this session plays.

docs.title: INTERRUPT_SPOKEN_AUDIO_AND_MIX_WITH_OTHERS = 'interruptSpokenAudioAndMixWithOthers'
docs.text:
Pause spoken audio content from other sessions when your app plays its audio.

Available from iOS 9.0.

docs.title: MIX_WITH_OTHERS = 'mixWithOthers'
docs.text: Whether audio from this session mixes with audio from active sessions in other audio apps.

docs.title: OVERRIDE_MUTED_MICROPHONE_INTERRUPTION = 'overrideMutedMicrophoneInterruption'
docs.text:
System interrupts the audio session when it mutes the built-in microphone.

Available from iOS 14.5.

docs.title: IosRecorderConfiguration
docs.text: iOS specific configuration for recording.

docs.title: manage_audio_session: bool = True
docs.text:
Whether to manage the shared AVAudioSession.

Set this to false if another plugin is already managing the AVAudioSession.

docs.title: options: list[IosAudioCategoryOption] = field(default_factory=lambda: [IosAudioCategoryOption.DEFAULT_TO_SPEAKER, IosAudioCategoryOption.ALLOW_BLUETOOTH, IosAudioCategoryOption.ALLOW_BLUETOOTH_A2DP])
docs.text: Optional audio behaviors.
